chunks.page_summary: This page details the integration of Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors with the Ecolab 3D TRASAR controller, outlining the involved products, purpose, and supported communication protocols.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Rheonics integration with Ecolab-3D Trasar-Modbus
Modified on: Fri, 2 May, 2025 at 5:56 PM

What products are involved?
This article is based on using the SME (Smart Module Electronics) from SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/), and DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/).

What is the purpose of this article?
This article provides guidance on integrating the SME inline viscometer with a 3D TRASAR (https://www.ecolab.com/offerings/3d-trasar-technology-for-cooling-water) controller (Ecolab's smart water management technology designed to monitor, control, and optimize industrial and commercial water treatment systems in real time) [1]

Figure 1. 3D Trasar diagram acting as a Modbus Master

Rheonics Type-SR sensors deliver robust, maintenance-free, Real-time, stable, repeatable, and reproducible measurements in Newtonian & non-Newtonian fluids, even in challenging environments, complementing the real-time monitoring and automation strengths of 3D TRASAR.

Supported protocols for Rheonics Type-SR include:
*   Modbus RTU (serial communication)
*   Modbus TCP/IP (Ethernet-based)
*   Analog 4-20 mA output
*   Profinet and Ethernet/IP

These communication options ensure flexibility to match the configuration and requirements of different 3D TRASAR system models.
key_facts: The article provides guidance on integrating the Rheonics SME inline viscometer with an Ecolab 3D TRASAR controller.
Ecolab's 3D TRASAR is a smart water management technology designed to monitor, control, and optimize industrial and commercial water treatment systems in real time.
Rheonics Type-SR sensors deliver robust, maintenance-free, real-time, stable, repeatable, and reproducible measurements in Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluids.
Rheonics Type-SR sensors can perform measurements even in challenging environments.
The 3D TRASAR system offers real-time monitoring and automation strengths.
Supported communication protocols for Rheonics Type-SR sensors include Modbus RTU (serial communication), Modbus TCP/IP (Ethernet-based), Analog 4-20 mA output, Profinet, and Ethernet/IP.
These communication options provide flexibility to match the configuration and requirements of different 3D TRASAR system models.
The 3D TRASAR device is shown acting as a Modbus Master.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the default Modbus RTU configuration for the Rheonics SME-TRD sensor and details how to modify these settings using the Rheonics Control Panel software, in addition to listing commonly used Modbus input registers for sensor data.
chunks.extracted_text:
1. Sensor Settings

The default configuration of the SME-TRD(Modbus RTU) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) using Modbus can be found in Table 1 below.
The configuration can be modified using the RCP software (Figure 1).

Table 1. Modbus default configuration parameters
| Modbus parameters | Value |
|-------------------|-------|
| Slave ID          | 1     |
| baudrate          | 38400 |
| Parity            | odd   |

Figure 2. RCP software and the communication tab are used for Modbus RTU configuration.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.5.1.6

UI Tabs: Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Diagnostics | Communication | Temp. Control | Logger | Help

Modbus RTU (RS-485) Section:
Modbus Address: 1
Baud Rate: 38400
Parity: odd

LCD Display (SME-TRD Only) Section:
Line 1 Parameter: Viscosity, Resolution: 0.00
Line 2 Parameter: Density, Resolution: 0.000
Line 3 Parameter: Temperature, Resolution: 0.00

Write and Update Section:
Write Config (button)
Load Config (button)

Ethernet Section:
Ethernet MAC: 70:B3:D5:D2:03:A3
DHCP: False
IP: 192.168.3.6
Subnet: 255.255.255.0
Gateway IP: 0.0.0.0
DNS: 0.0.0.0
Terminal Port: 10001

Location ID Section:
Location ID: [empty field]
Location Name: [empty field]
Latitude: 0.000000
Longitude: 0.000000
Altitude: 0

Operation History Section:
RTC Time: 19/09/24 11:27:39
Last power Reset: 16/09/24 16:08:22
Supply Voltage: 23.26 V
SME Temp: 37.60 °C

2. Recommended registers:

Sensor data is available in the input registers. (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297459-modbus-input-registers). Table below shows the commonly used registers.

Table of commonly used input registers:
| Parameter    | Description         | Internal Address | Modbus Register Address (3xxxx) | Data Type | Interpretation                 |
|--------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------|
| Parameter 12 | Viscosity Last Good | 136              | 300137                          | 2Float32  | Parameter 12 value as float    |
| Parameter 13 | Density Last Good   | 144              | 300145                          | 2Float32  | Parameter 13 value as float    |
| Parameter 2  | Temperature median  | 56               | 300057                          | 2Float32  | Parameter 2 value as float     |
key_facts: The Rheonics SME-TRD sensor utilizes Modbus RTU for communication.
The default Modbus RTU configuration for the SME-TRD is Slave ID 1, baudrate 38400, and odd parity.
Sensor configuration, including Modbus RTU and Ethernet settings, can be modified using the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) software, specifically version 5.5.1.6.
The RCP software allows users to configure parameters displayed on the LCD, such as viscosity, density, and temperature, along with their display resolutions.
Ethernet settings configurable via RCP include MAC address, DHCP, IP, Subnet, Gateway IP, DNS, and Terminal Port.
The RCP software provides operational history, displaying RTC Time, Last power Reset time, current Supply Voltage, and SME Temperature.
Sensor data like Viscosity Last Good, Density Last Good, and Temperature median are available through Modbus input registers at specified addresses (e.g., 300137 for Viscosity Last Good).
The data type for the recommended input registers (Viscosity, Density, Temperature) is 2Float32, which is interpreted as a float value.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the location and configuration requirements of the Modbus RTU RS-485 port on a Rheonics SME-TRD device.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. Where is the Modbus RTU RS-485 Port located?
The RS-485 interface is located between the power input port and the Ethernet port. It uses a 2-wire RS-485 configuration (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297458-connecting-modbus-rtu-rs-485-outputs). The device does not include internal biasing or termination resistors, so proper RS-485 network setup—including external biasing and termination—must be ensured for reliable communication.
Figure 3. SME-TRD. Modbus RTU port.
key_facts: The RS-485 interface is located between the power input port and the Ethernet port.
It uses a 2-wire RS-485 configuration.
The device does not include internal biasing or termination resistors.
Proper RS-485 network setup, including external biasing and termination, must be ensured for reliable communication.
warnings_notes: The device does not include internal biasing or termination resistors, so proper RS-485 network setup—including external biasing and termination—must be ensured for reliable communication.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the 3D Trasar Controller's LED indicators and guides the user on how to navigate its touch screen to the Modbus inputs option.
chunks.extracted_text:
4. 3D Trasar Controller Settings
4.1.Controller touch screen

Green LED: controller powered, booted and power stable.
Blinking green LED: controller powered, booted and power unstable.
Red LED: active alarm, action required.
Blue LED: gateway linked and connected to Nalco server.
Blinking blue LED (1/sec): gateway linked, connected to Nalco server, but signal strength is low.
Blinking blue LED (5/sec): gateway linked, but not connected to Nalco server.
Green LED: Subscription service active.
Blinking green LED: Subscription in progress (learning mode).

Figure 5. Controller touch panel options.

4.2. Go to the configure menu and select the Modbus inputs option

Configure
Control & Alarm
Sensors
Analog/Digital
Communications
SCADA/DCS
Modbus Inputs

Figure 6. Accessing the Modbus Data.
key_facts: The 3D Trasar Controller's LEDs indicate power status, alarm conditions, gateway connection to the Nalco server, and subscription service status.
Different blinking patterns of the blue LED (1/sec vs. 5/sec) distinguish between low signal strength and no connection to the Nalco server.
To access Modbus settings, users must navigate to the 'Configure' menu and select the 'Modbus Inputs' option on the touch screen.
The 24/7 icon's green LED indicates an active subscription service, while a blinking green LED indicates the subscription is in progress (learning mode).

Variant 1 illustrates a straightforward connection between the RS-485 ports of both devices. The 3D TRASAR must be configured to poll data from the SME using the register addresses specified in section 2.

Figure 8. Wiring diagram 3D Trasar and Rheonics SME-Modbus RTU

6. Wiring 2-2 SME into a single 3D Trasar
In this setup, both SME devices are connected to a single Ethernet switch, with one Ethernet cable running from the Ethernet switch to the 3D TRASAR. Each SME must be assigned a unique static IP address to ensure proper communication.
key_facts: Modbus inputs, including LAN and serial interfaces, are configurable through a dedicated navigation menu.
The system supports communication via both Modbus TCP (Ethernet) and Modbus RTU (RS-485).
For a direct RS-485 connection between a 3D TRASAR and an SME, the 3D TRASAR needs to be configured to poll data using specific register addresses (as per section 2, not provided).
The controller's circuit board includes a replaceable BR2032 battery.
Connecting multiple SME devices to a single 3D TRASAR via an Ethernet switch requires each SME to have a unique static IP address for proper communication.
diagram_labels: Circuit board contains one replaceable battery type BR2032
warnings_notes: The 3D TRASAR must be configured to poll data from the SME using the register addresses specified in section 2.
Each SME must be assigned a unique static IP address to ensure proper communication.

7. Resources
1. https://www.ecolab.com/offerings/3d-trasar
2. https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412493-modbus-rs485-to-usb-converter-compatible-with-rheonics-sme

key_facts: The Controller circuit board contains one replaceable battery of type BR2032.
Rheonics SME devices can be connected to a controller via an Ethernet switch.
Both SME 1 and SME 2 units are powered by a 24VDC supply.
SME units support Modbus RTU RS485 and Modbus TCP Ethernet RJ45 communication protocols.
The wiring for SME units utilizes 4x2x24AWG shielded cables terminated with M12 8-pin female A-coded connectors.
The system configuration depicted is identified as 'Option 2' for connecting Rheonics SME with a 3D Trasar system.
External resources are provided for 3D Trasar information and Rheonics support articles regarding Modbus RS485 to USB converters and solution folders.

Figure 9. 3D Trasar with two Rheonics SME connected to an Ethernet switch. Option 2
warnings_notes: A recommended 500mA slow-blow fuse should be used for the SME 2 power supply (implied for SME 1 as well due to identical power supply block).
The circuit board in the controller contains a replaceable BR2032 battery.
